mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, reducing recession risk and supporting equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings and lower equity risk premiums over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2012 payroll level 132989k indicates post-crisis recovery phase
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate below 0.25% signals zero lower bound monetary accommodation, which compresses equity discount rates and increases duration-sensitive equity valuations. This mechanism operates continuously during the accommodation regime and predicts positive 5-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% as of April 2012 indicates sustained zero-bound policy
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or correlation between FEDFUNDS level and 5-day returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening aggregate demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations. The interaction effect amplifies the negative signal relative to either series alone over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% and RSAFS declines > 3% over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 654k and RSAFS at 408039M as of March 2012 reflect post-crisis demand normalization
regime_hint: late-cycle demand deceleration with synchronized weakness across housing and consumption
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (HOUST × RSAFS) has lower predictive power than either series independently (lower Rank IC by > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) growth above 0.5% month-over-month indicates accelerating supply-side capacity utilization and productivity, which supports profit margins and equity valuations over the next 20 trading days in non-recessionary regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 96.57 in March 2012 shows post-crisis industrial recovery
regime_hint: expansion phase with positive supply-side momentum
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 54% of months with INDPRO growth > 0.5%, or Rank IC is below 0.025 over any rolling 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% year-over-year triggers expectations of future monetary tightening, which increases equity discount rates and produces negative 60-day forward returns as investors reprice growth and duration risk.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 229.18 in April 2012 reflects contained inflation in post-crisis period
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle or inflation acceleration regime
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in more than 48% of periods when CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 2.5%, or correlation between inflation rate and 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 8.0% signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, which reduces equity valuations through lower expected earnings and higher equity risk premiums. The mechanism operates with a 5-day lag as market repricing occurs.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.1% in April 2012 indicates persistent post-crisis labor slack
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of observations when UNRATE > 8.0%, or the relationship between UNRATE level and 5-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion that supports equity earnings growth. This always-on mechanism predicts positive 20-day returns in expansion regimes independent of other conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13502.4B in Q4 2011 reflects post-crisis recovery trajectory
regime_hint: economic expansion with positive real growth
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 56% of quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5%, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint weakness in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) combined with elevated unemployment (UNRATE > 8.0%) creates a synchronized demand-and-labor shock that amplifies negative equity valuations. The three-way interaction predicts negative 60-day returns more strongly than any single series.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% AND HOUST declines > 3% AND UNRATE > 8.0% simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:All three series show post-crisis normalization with persistent slack as of May 2012
regime_hint: synchronized demand and labor market weakness in recovery phase
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the three-way interaction term has lower predictive power than the best two-way interaction (Rank IC difference > 0.04).
